title: Daily Market Digest: April 3, 2026
subtitle: US Markets Closed for Good Friday; Geopolitical Tensions and Jobs Report in Focus

items.headline: US Markets Closed for Good Friday Amid Geopolitical Concerns and Strong Jobs Data
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are closed today for the Good Friday holiday, leaving investors to digest a stronger-than-expected March jobs report and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Futures for major indices showed slight declines in quiet trading. Overall market sentiment remains cautious, heavily influenced by the Iran war and its impact on oil prices, alongside persistent inflation worries.

items.headline: Franklin Covey Co. Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Franklin Covey Co. is noted as a top performer for April 2026 so far, with a monthly gain of 44.4%.

items.headline: SBA Communications Up on April 2nd
items.summary: SBA Communications Corporation saw a significant rise, closing up 18.93% on April 2, 2026.

items.headline: Lipocine Inc. Plunges on Trial Results
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. experienced a severe decline, down 77.84% in early April, following the report of topline safety and efficacy results for its drug LPCN 1154, which missed its primary endpoint.

items.headline: PMGC Holdings Sees Pre-Market Drop
items.summary: PMGC Holdings showed a pre-market decline of approximately 59% on April 3, attributed to a sharp reversal after recent gains and potential profit-taking.

items.headline: Strong March Jobs Report Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 178,000 nonfarm payrolls in March, significantly surpassing the consensus forecast of 60,000. The unemployment rate also edged down to 4.3%. This robust data reinforces expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates, pushing back against the likelihood of near-term rate cuts.

items.headline: Energy and Defensive Sectors Lead Amid Caution
items.summary: On April 3rd, defensive sectors like Utilities ($XLU) and Consumer Staples ($XLP) continued to outperform, signaling a cautious stance among investors favoring stability. Energy ($XLE) and Materials ($XLB) also showed leadership, while Technology ($XLK) and Consumer Discretionary ($XLY) were observed lagging. This rotation reflects ongoing economic uncertainty.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge as Iran War Fuels Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Oil prices continued their upward trend, with Brent crude nearing $110 per barrel, driven by persistent worries of a prolonged Iran war and potential disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This surge in energy costs is a key factor in the worsening inflation outlook, with economists now projecting 2026 inflation to be in the 3%-4% range.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Plunges to Near-Historic Low
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index for April 3, 2026, plummeted to 53.3, marking its third-lowest reading in 75 years. This significant drop reflects growing household concern over the economic fallout from the Middle East conflict, including skyrocketing energy costs and a volatile stock market.
